summary: Refuse send without --yes whenever stdin is non-TTY OR --stdin was passed (which drains stdin for the body), so the confirmation prompt can never silently EOF-cancel a send.
reason: Greptile P1 findings on PR #676, fifth and sixth passes: (1) `cat body.json | … --stdin` — stdout was still a TTY but stdin was a pipe at EOF, so the old stdout-based check let Fscanln read empty and cancel silently. (2) Interactive TTY + `--stdin` — even with a terminal, io.ReadAll(os.Stdin) drains the body, leaving Fscanln nothing to read. The combined check (stdinBody || !stdinIsTTY → require --yes) covers both vectors with one actionable error.
